description:
You are helping a technical writer who is preparing release-notes statistics.
    Inside the workstation you will find the following directory that already contains
    several plain-text application logs:

        /home/user/docs/logs/

    Goal:
    1. Scan **every “*.log” file** inside /home/user/docs/logs/ (do not recurse into sub-directories).
    2. Count how many lines in each file contain the exact, **case-sensitive** keywords
       “ERROR”, “WARNING”, and “INFO”.
    3. Write the results to a new comma-separated values (CSV) file located at

        /home/user/docs/analysis/2023-operations-summary.csv

       • Create the /home/user/docs/analysis/ directory if it does not yet exist.  
       • The CSV file **must overwrite any existing file of the same name**.  
       • Use LF line endings.

    Required CSV format (the automated grader will compare this exactly; make sure
    to match spacing and punctuation precisely):

        file,errors,warnings,infos
        <filename>,<ERROR count>,<WARNING count>,<INFO count>
        …(one data row per “*.log” file, sorted alphabetically by filename)…

    Example of a correctly-formatted file (the numbers below are illustrative only):

        file,errors,warnings,infos
        app1.log,2,1,5
        app2.log,0,3,7
        syslog.log,4,0,0

    4. When the CSV is finished, list its contents on the terminal so the writer can
       copy-paste it quickly.

    Hints (you are free to solve the task any way you like):
    • Standard tools such as grep, wc, cut, sort, echo, printf, etc. are sufficient.
    • Only 3-4 shell commands are needed in total; brevity is fine as long as the
      final CSV exactly matches the prescribed format.

    Deliverables checked by the grader:
    • The file /home/user/docs/analysis/2023-operations-summary.csv must exist.
    • Its contents must match the required CSV layout and counts.
    • Directory names and paths are case-sensitive.
truth:
Initial state (already present before the agent starts):

    1. Directory: /home/user/docs/logs/

       ├── app1.log  
       ├── app2.log  
       └── app3.log  

    2. Exact contents of each log file (final newline included):

       /home/user/docs/logs/app1.log  
       --------------------------------
       INFO Starting application
       WARNING Disk space almost full
       ERROR Failed to open configuration
       INFO Retrying startup
       ERROR Aborted
       (newline)

       /home/user/docs/logs/app2.log  
       --------------------------------
       INFO Initialising
       INFO Running main loop
       INFO Shutdown complete
       (newline)

       /home/user/docs/logs/app3.log  
       --------------------------------
       ERROR Crash detected
       WARNING Possible memory leak
       ERROR Stack trace follows
       WARNING Memory leak suspected
       INFO Attempting restart
       INFO Restart successful
       (newline)

    Expected final artefact created by the agent:

       /home/user/docs/analysis/2023-operations-summary.csv  
       ------------------------------------------------------
       file,errors,warnings,infos
       app1.log,2,1,2
       app2.log,0,0,3
       app3.log,2,2,2
       (newline)

    Verification checklist for the grader:
    • The analysis directory exists and is not empty.
    • The CSV file path is exactly /home/user/docs/analysis/2023-operations-summary.csv.
    • The CSV contains four lines in total (header + 3 data lines).
    • Lines are sorted alphabetically by filename.
    • Counts match the ground-truth above (ERROR, WARNING, INFO occurrences).
    • File ends with a single LF newline.
